rawDescription: I recently interviewed at Google for a Software Engineer position. During the phone-screen, the interviewer asked me to find the maximum sum of a contiguous subarray in an array of integers. Time limit O(N). The input size could be up to 10^5, so an efficient solution was required. I wrote the code in Python and the interviewer was satisfied.

I recently interviewed at Meta for a Backend Engineer position. During the onsite, the interviewer asked me to find the longest substring without repeating characters. Space complexity should be O(min(N, M)). The input size could be up to 10^5, so an efficient solution was required. I wrote the code in Python and the interviewer was satisfied.

I recently interviewed at Amazon for a Frontend Engineer position. During the online-assessment, the interviewer asked me to merge two sorted linked lists into a single sorted linked list in O(N) time. The input size could be up to 10^5, so an efficient solution was required. I wrote the code in Python and the interviewer was satisfied.

I recently interviewed at Netflix for a Fullstack Engineer position. During the phone-screen, the interviewer asked me to check if a given string is a valid palindrome, considering only alphanumeric characters and ignoring cases. The input size could be up to 10^5, so an efficient solution was required. I wrote the code in Python and the interviewer was satisfied.

I recently interviewed at Apple for a SRE position. During the onsite, the interviewer asked me to find the maximum depth of a binary tree. The input size could be up to 10^5, so an efficient solution was required. I wrote the code in Python and the interviewer was satisfied.

I recently interviewed at Microsoft for a Software Engineer position. During the online-assessment, the interviewer asked me to invert a binary tree. The input size could be up to 10^5, so an efficient solution was required. I wrote the code in Python and the interviewer was satisfied.
